channel.title: Posts on Stori Wyllt 2026
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Stori Wyllt 2026

item.title: Workshop No.1 at Ysgol Gynradd Aberaeron
item.description: Oriel Gallery Seven-spot Ladybird caught in the net

item.title: Tŵr Gwyllt at Aberaeron
item.description: Guest post by artist Rebecca Wyn Kelly: While Jake was visiting the Jewsons yard with Ysgol Aberaeron, we came across this toad tucked beneath a log, a quiet little resident already making use of the space. I’m really hoping he might take shelter in one of the Lletau Llyffant I’ve created; these are small shelters made with the toads in mind. A little about Llyffantod In old Welsh folk belief, toads were linked to wise women and hidden knowledge, and were said to carry carreg y llyffant in their heads, a magical stone with protective powers.

item.title: Aberaeron site reccie
item.description: Last year, we ran the Grassland ID workshops at Penmorfa with Ysgol Gynradd Aberaeron. Students from Ysgol Gynradd Aberaeron on Grassland ID workshop 2025 This year, we’re hoping for another magical spot, with a witch’s house, but this has yet to be confirmed. I had a chance meeting with my colleague, Tree Officer Norman Birch, and he explained that there is a public foot path in Aberaeron that runs on the south bank of the river Aeron, past the allotments an up to Jewsons.

item.title: Ysgol Llanilar site visit
item.description: I Looking westward along the river Ystwyth

item.title: Stori Wyllt Symposiwm
item.description: Croeso i’r Stori Wyllt symposiwm! Welcome to the Stori Wyllt symposium! The Stori Wyllt Symposiwm was an ideal opportunity for the teachers, naturalists and creatives from the four schools to meet each other in-person and share ideas at a central location in Ceredigion (Moody Meadows at Bargoed Farm ar bwys Llwyncelyn). The Stori Wyllt schools are, from the south to the north, Ysgol Gynradd Aberteifi, Ysgol Llanarth, Ysgol Gynradd Aberaeron and Ysgol Llanilar.
